title: Dense Egyptian Green Beans Stir‑fry
description: A resinous, velvety stir-fry of fresh green beans cured in shio‑koji, finished with sesame oil, lime, and a touch of heat - a solo Egyptian dinner that feels indulgent yet plant-friendly.
instructions: Heat the wok over medium-high heat and drizzle in sesame oil until shimmering.
Add the minced garlic, sliced onion, and grated ginger, sautéing until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Stir in the green beans, tossing to coat with the aromatics.
Sprinkle shio-koji and soy sauce over the beans, then stir vigorously to distribute the cure.
Pour in lime juice, olive oil, and optionally chili flakes, then continue cooking for 4-5 minutes until beans are tender yet crisp.
Taste and adjust seasoning, adding a splash more soy sauce if needed.
Remove from heat and scatter toasted sesame seeds over the beans.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro for brightness.
Serve immediately, pairing with a bowl of steamed rice or flatbread.
Enjoy the resinous, velvety texture that melts in the mouth while keeping the dish flexible for vegetarians.
